failedAssertions.reason: Results table never renders. get_values=True fix (f1fe82ab) is correctly deployed in app.py. However, responses.py line 85 crashes with KeyError: 1 in serializeVBR — uses integer label access image_row[1] on a pandas Series whose index is string-typed after reset_index(). Fix: change image_row[1] to image_row.iloc[1] in responses.py. This blocks ALL queries that produce VBR/ExplicitVBR columns (all Destrieux queries). Verified: 5/5 query executions fail with this error in backend logs.
Blocked by same serializeVBR KeyError as VAL-EXEC-005. Results table never renders, so symbol selector cannot appear.
Blocked by same serializeVBR KeyError as VAL-EXEC-005. Results table never renders, so column sort cannot be tested.
Blocked by same serializeVBR KeyError as VAL-EXEC-005. Results table never renders, so pagination cannot be tested.
Blocked by same serializeVBR KeyError as VAL-EXEC-005. Results table never renders, so CSV download button is never shown.

rootCauses.description: responses.py serializeVBR function (line 85) uses integer label access image_row[1] on a pandas Series. After reset_index(), the Series index contains string column names (e.g., ['index', 's'] for a relation with column 's'). Integer label access [1] raises KeyError since the index is not integer-based. This crash occurs inside send_query_update callback for every query that produces VBR columns, preventing any WebSocket completion message from being sent to the frontend.
rootCauses.suggestedFix: In responses.py, change line 85 from 'vbr = image_row[1]' to 'vbr = image_row.iloc[1]' to use positional (iloc) instead of label-based access.

appliedUpdates.description: Updated Known UI behaviors for execution milestone: marked VAL-EXEC-011 cancel bug as fixed, documented new serializeVBR KeyError blocker for results table assertions.
